commentary_text: The market assigns Portugal a 52% win probability versus the model's 35%, a 17-percentage-point gap favoring Portugal that dwarfs the model's near-parity assessment. The sportsbook and Polymarket consensus align closely, suggesting real-money flows are driving conviction toward Portugal that the model's Elo and goal-distribution framework does not capture. That gap could reflect recent lineup changes, injury news, or current form trends unavailable to the model at its last update. The dashboard surfaces this as a notable signal: either Portugal has strengthened or Colombia weakened in ways the statistical baseline has yet to absorb, or the market is overweighting one side on incomplete information. Neither interpretation is a verdict.
